title: Celebs Take the Stage in 'Chicago' on Broadway: Biggest Hits and Notable Flops Revealed
body:
The iconic musical Chicago has been a staple of Broadway for decades, with its sassy humor, catchy tunes, and of course, a cast of glamorous stars who have taken on the iconic roles of Roxie Hart and Velma Kelly. In a show known for predicting the next big thing, Chicago has had its fair share of both hits and flops over the years. Recently, Whitney Leigh Ross (not Levitt) set a new record for the show, breaking the previous mark for most performances as Roxie Hart. But with a cast that has included everyone from movie stars to pop sensations, not everyone has been a winner. In fact, some have been downright disastrous.

At the top of the list of Chicago's biggest hits is undoubtedly the incomparable Liza Minnelli, who originated the role of Sally Bowles in the 1975 film adaptation. Her electrifying performance helped launch her career and cemented the show's place in the pantheon of American musical theater. Other notable successes include the likes of Chita Rivera, who won a Tony Award for her performance as Velma Kelly in 1977, and Bebe Neuwirth, who won two Tony Awards for her performances as Roxie Hart and Velma Kelly in the 1990s. More recently, actresses like Renée Zellweger, who won an Oscar for her portrayal of Roxie in the 2002 film adaptation, and Catherine Zeta-Jones, who won an Oscar for her performance as Velma Kelly, have brought their own brand of stardom to the show.

However, not everyone has been as fortunate. Some of the most notable flops include the likes of Melanie Griffith, who played Roxie Hart in 1996 and received scathing reviews from critics. Griffith's performance was panned as " wooden" and "uninspired", and she was ultimately replaced by another actress. Brooke Shields, who played Roxie in 2000, also received negative reviews, with critics calling her performance "boring" and "unconvincing". Even pop sensation Christina Aguilera, who played the role of Velma Kelly in 2006, was met with lukewarm reception, with some critics calling her performance "over-the-top" and "trying too hard".

So what makes the difference between a hit and a flop in this iconic show? One reason is undoubtedly the chemistry between the leads. When the chemistry is there, the show is electric, but when it's not, the result is a lackluster performance that fails to capture the magic of the show. Another reason is the star's willingness to dive headfirst into the role and commit fully to the character. When an actress is fully invested in the part, it shines through on stage, and the audience responds.

As Chicago continues to delight audiences on Broadway, it's clear that the show's magic is not just about the stars, but about the timeless story, memorable music, and the talented cast and crew that bring it all together. With a new cast taking to the stage every few months, it's anyone's guess who will be the next big hit or flop in this iconic show. But one thing is for sure: Chicago will continue to be a must-see destination for theater lovers and fans of the iconic musical.

As the show continues to evolve and adapt to new talent and new times, it's clear that Chicago's secret to success lies not just in its ability to predict the next big thing, but in its ability to transcend time and trends, remaining a timeless classic that continues to captivate audiences to this day.
